**The Most Blessed Order of Setia Negara Brunei**[[1]](#cite_note-1) ([Malay](/wiki/Malay_language "Malay language"): *Darjah Setia Negara Brunei Yang Amat Bahagia*), also translated as **The Most Blessed Order of Loyalty to the State of Brunei**,[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) is an [order](/wiki/Order_(decoration) "Order (decoration)") of [Brunei Darussalam](/wiki/Brunei "Brunei"). It was established on 29 November 1959 by [Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III](/wiki/Sultan_Omar_Ali_Saifuddien_III "Sultan Omar Ali Saifuddien III").[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

## Current classes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Setia_Negara_Brunei&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Current classes")]

The four classes of appointment to the Order are, from highest grade to lowest:

| Class | [Post-nominal](/wiki/Post-nominal_letters "Post-nominal letters") | [Title](/wiki/Malay_styles_and_titles "Malay styles and titles") | [Ribbon bar](/wiki/Ribbon_bar "Ribbon bar") |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| First Class | PSNB | Dato Seri Setia |  |
| Second Class | DSNB | Dato Setia |  |
| Third Class | SNB | – |  |
| Fourth Class | PSB |  |

## Description

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Setia_Negara_Brunei&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Description")]

The First Class included a 100 millimetres (3.9 inches) sash badge with a long neck riband, silver-gilt and enamel, good very fine, and a star and crescent suspension measuring 67 millimetres (2.6 inches).[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Recipients

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Order_of_Setia_Negara_Brunei&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Recipients")]

For committed, honourable, faithful, and exceptional service. It is given to non-military personnel.[[4]](#cite_note-4)
